channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Riot Games Announces First Riftbound Ban List for League of Legends TCG
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced its first official ban list for its League of Legends TCG. The first Riftbound ban list will restrict the use of several cards in the game's Standard Constructed format. In addition, the developers have articulated their reasons for their first ban.

The First Riftbound Ban List Announcement

According to an official Riot Games blog post, the first Riftbound ban list will ban the following cards and battlefields from Constructed play effect March 31, 2026.

  • Called Shot
  • Draven, Vanquisher
  • Fight or Flight
  • Scrapheap
  • Battlefields
  • Disarming Tree
  • Obelisk of Power
  • Reaver's Row

The rest of the blog post is dedicated to the reasoning behind this ban list. It opens stating that the ban list is a work in progress. This is to be expected with shifting competitive metas in any card game, be it Riftbound or Magic: The Gathering. 

The guiding principles behind the Riftbound ban list are summarized into several parts. These include questions about the impact of the card. Is the card unhealthily overrepresented? Will the cards' problems get worse over time? Does the card promote play patterns that negatively impact the game?

The other parts are more of a design philosophy to which  the team is maintaining. One is not changing the game's core rules or just changing the card's effects via errata. The other is outright banning cards instead of restricting how many copies can be in a deck.

This is for several reasons. One is if the developers starting changing rules now, it would be devastating to the long-term health of Riftbound, especially when each new set like Vendetta introduces new gameplay mechanics. The second is banning cards rather than restricting them prevents the problem getting worse through dedicated magnified randomness.

The final part of the blog post goes into the exact details behind the banned cards. Called Shot and Scrapheap are banned since they are part of what's called "Miracle Decks" in the community, which lets players draw and play large numbers of cards at discounted costs. Draven is banned due to him being a power outlier, an outright mistake according to the developers.

Fight or Flight was banned to limit the power of Chaos decks. As for battlefields, Reaver's Row is banned due to its synergy with Draven and due to it encouraging noninteractive play patterns. Dreaming Tree provides substantial card flow, which can easily tip the scale towards certain deck styles. Lastly, Obelisk of Power is banned due to its ubiquity in competitive decks.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/riot-games-announces-first-riftbound-ban-list-for-league-of-legends-tcg
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Games Workshop Announces Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition and New Armageddon Box Set
item.content:encoded:
A new edition of Games Workshop's grimdark sci-fi miniatures game is coming. Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition is set to bring new rules, while retaining elements fans love, and will launch alongside a new box set featuring the Space Marines and the Orks.

The Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition Announcement

The announcement of Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition happened over the weekend during AdeptiCon 2026. The key parts of that announcement were then posted on an official Warhammer-Community post. The post breaks down several elements of the new edition: the new rules, the new lore of the Warhammer 40,000 setting, and the contents of the new Armageddon box.

The lore of the new edition puts it after the events of Armageddon: The Return of Yarrick on the titular planet. An Ork vanguard, lead by Wazdakka Gutsmek, has landed and devastated Imperial forces. In response to Commissar Yarrick plea for aid, the Imperium has started Operation Imperator, which has deployed a task force of Space Marines from different Chapters to the planet for aid.

The Armageddon box set lets you play out this world-spanning battle. It contains new miniatures for Space Marines, including new Intercessor squads which blend both new and old design elements. The new Ork Boyz miniatures have gotten similar upgrades, now entering battle with a choppa, slugga, and shoota into battle.

As for the new rules in Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition, Games Workshop were more measured in how much was new. The post states all current codexes and faction rules for the 10th Edition of the game will still be valid. Given that the 10th edition of the game is a little over two years old, that can be a relief for those who spent so much money on new books and models.

So what has changed? Detachment rules are now more flexible. You can now choose multiple detachments to make up your army. Furthermore, mission objectives will be dictated by which detachments you bring into battle. Defensive armies will gain points for holding ground, more aggressive armies will gain points by how much they kill, etc..

In addition, Warhammer 40,000 11th Edition will be making other general changes. Objectives won't be marked by circles but by terrain footprints. Terrain rules are changed where cover bonus only impacts Hit rolls, not saves. Lastly, Combat has been streamlined, allowing more flexibility for picking targets while charging, changes to activation order, and changes to the order of consolidation and pile-in rules.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/games-workshop-announces-warhammer-40000-11th-edition-and-new-armageddon-box-set
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
